evals.prompt: 看下 task `2026-04-26-task-journal-redesign-a3` 现在到哪了
evals.expected_output: Reads `.agents/tasks/2026-04-26-task-journal-redesign-a3/journal.md` (or uses the journal.py read helper) and summarizes the latest entries. The path convention `.agents/tasks/<task-id>/journal.md` is followed and `brief.md` is treated as immutable context, not a place to look for current status.

assertions.name: uses_journal_path_convention
assertions.description: Output reads the journal at `.agents/tasks/<task-id>/journal.md`, not docs/tasks/ or any task-state-management path

assertions.name: treats_journal_as_status_source
assertions.description: Output derives current status from journal entries rather than from brief.md or memory

evals.prompt: 我现在还有哪些任务在跑？看下 .agents/tasks/ 下面有什么
evals.expected_output: Lists subdirectories under `.agents/tasks/`, filters by activity using either the last journal entry timestamp or directory mtime, and treats the absence of a `done:` entry (or a recent non-done entry) as the activity signal. Does not assume `active/` vs `completed/` partitioning.

assertions.name: uses_flat_layout
assertions.description: Output respects the flat layout under `.agents/tasks/` and does not look for active/completed subdirs

assertions.name: uses_journal_for_activity_signal
assertions.description: Output uses last journal entry or `done:` presence to judge activity, not a state-machine status field

evals.prompt: 帮我看看任务记录有没有漂移
evals.expected_output: Invokes `python3 ${SKILL_DIR}/scripts/lint_tasks.py --tasks-dir .agents/tasks` (or an equivalent absolute path to the installed skill) and reports the error/warn output. Does not invent a `mise run lint-tasks` or other wrapper command, and does not try to write its own checker.

assertions.name: invokes_lint_script_directly
assertions.description: Output calls `python3 .../scripts/lint_tasks.py` directly with `--tasks-dir`, not via mise or a custom wrapper

assertions.name: reports_severity_levels
assertions.description: Output distinguishes error vs warn from the linter rather than collapsing to a single pass/fail

evals.prompt: 我刚作为 design-structure 完成了一个设计树的初稿，文件保存在 docs/design-tree/2026-04-26-task-journal-redesign.md，对应 task id 是 2026-04-26-task-journal-redesign-a3。把这件事登记到 task journal。
evals.expected_output: Appends a single journal entry to `.agents/tasks/2026-04-26-task-journal-redesign-a3/journal.md` with heading `## <ISO8601> — design-structure`, a `saved:` line naming the design-tree file, and a short body. Uses the reserved key `saved` (not a synonym), keeps the body within 15 lines, and does not modify `brief.md`.

assertions.name: uses_reserved_saved_key
assertions.description: Entry uses the reserved `saved:` key for a persistent artifact, not a custom synonym

assertions.name: follows_entry_format
assertions.description: Heading is `## <ISO8601> — <skill-name>` with the em dash, followed by at least one key:value line

assertions.name: leaves_brief_untouched
assertions.description: Output does not edit brief.md as part of this milestone log

evals.prompt: PR #142 合到 main 了，把 task `2026-04-26-task-journal-redesign-a3` 标完成
evals.expected_output: Appends a `done:` entry under the task's journal.md with a value such as `merged` and a short body referencing PR #142. Does NOT move or rename `.agents/tasks/2026-04-26-task-journal-redesign-a3/` (no migration to completed/, no archival rename).

assertions.name: appends_done_entry
assertions.description: Output appends an entry with reserved key `done:` and a non-empty value

assertions.name: does_not_move_directory
assertions.description: Output does not propose moving, renaming, or archiving the task directory; it stays under `.agents/tasks/<id>/`

evals.prompt: task-state-management 当时为什么用 ownership lease？这个设计动机是什么？
evals.expected_output: Treats this as a historical/design-rationale question about the retired skill. Does not read or write any `.agents/tasks/` records, does not invoke task-journal scripts, and does not propose journal entries. May reference the ADR or git history, but does not activate task-journal.

assertions.name: no_journal_io
assertions.description: Output does not perform any read/write under `.agents/tasks/` for this question

assertions.name: scoped_to_history
assertions.description: Output stays scoped to the historical question and does not graft on a task-journal workflow

evals.prompt: docs/exec-plans/ 下面这些文件的命名应该改成什么？现在有点乱
evals.expected_output: Treats this as a docs convention / file-naming question. Does not invoke task-journal, does not propose creating a task directory, and does not append journal entries. May reference docs/README.md naming conventions but does not activate task-journal.

assertions.name: no_task_creation
assertions.description: Output does not create a `.agents/tasks/<id>/` directory for this question

assertions.name: no_journal_append
assertions.description: Output does not write any journal entry for this docs-only question

evals.prompt: 怎么写好的 git commit message？有什么通用规范吗？
evals.expected_output: Treats this as a general writing/methodology question. Does not invoke task-journal, does not reference `.agents/tasks/`, and does not propose creating or updating any task record.

assertions.name: no_task_journal_reference
assertions.description: Output does not bring up task-journal or `.agents/tasks/` for a general best-practices question

evals.prompt: 帮我把这个想法整理成一个任务：我想给我们的内部工具加一个聊天机器人，能查 PR 状态、回顾上周提交、提醒我有哪些 review 没回复
evals.expected_output: Recognizes this as a task-creation request and routes to `task-brief`. Does NOT directly create `.agents/tasks/<id>/` from task-journal — task-journal is not the creation entry point. May mention that once the brief is confirmed, task-brief will be the one to create the directory and write the first journal entry.

assertions.name: routes_to_task_brief
assertions.description: Output recognizes task-brief as the creation entry point, not task-journal

assertions.name: does_not_self_create_directory
assertions.description: Output does not create `.agents/tasks/<id>/` from task-journal alone

evals.prompt: 把 src/utils/format.ts 里的变量 `tmp` 重命名为 `buffer`
evals.expected_output: Performs the rename directly. Does not create a task directory, does not propose journal entries, and does not invoke task-journal at all. A trivial single-step action does not warrant task records.

assertions.name: no_task_creation_for_trivial_action
assertions.description: Output does not propose creating `.agents/tasks/<id>/` for a one-step rename

evals.prompt: 你现在以 design-readiness-check 的身份，对 task `2026-04-26-task-journal-redesign-a3` 的当前设计做完了一次审查，结论是 ready。把这条结论登记到 journal。
evals.expected_output: Appends an entry with heading `## <ISO8601> — design-readiness-check`, the reserved key `readiness: ready`, and an optional short body (≤ 15 lines). The reserved key has a value from {ready, not-ready}, not a free-form synonym like 'good' or 'pass'.

assertions.name: readiness_uses_canonical_value
assertions.description: Entry value for `readiness:` is exactly `ready` or `not-ready`, not a paraphrase

assertions.name: format_compliance
assertions.description: Heading uses the em dash separator, includes ISO 8601 timestamp, and is followed by at least one key:value line

assertions.name: body_within_limit
assertions.description: Body, if present, stays within 15 lines; long content is offloaded to artifacts/

evals.prompt: task `2026-04-26-task-journal-redesign-a3` 已经有几条 journal entry 了。我现在想把 brief.md 的 User Goal 改一下，因为最初目标写得太宽。
evals.expected_output: Refuses to directly edit brief.md after the first journal entry has been written. Instead, guides the user to record the change as a new journal entry using a key like `refined:` (or `note:`) describing the goal narrowing. Cites the brief immutability rule from the SKILL.md.

assertions.name: refuses_direct_brief_edit
assertions.description: Output does not edit brief.md; it explains why and routes the change to a journal entry instead

assertions.name: proposes_refined_entry
assertions.description: Output proposes a journal entry with a key such as `refined:` capturing the goal change

evals.prompt: 在我现在这个项目里跑一下 task journal 的 lint，看看有没有破损
evals.expected_output: Calls `python3 ${SKILL_DIR}/scripts/lint_tasks.py --tasks-dir .agents/tasks` (with the actual installed skill path resolved). Does NOT invoke `mise run check-task-journal` (that is the loom repo's internal test runner, not a per-project linter), does not invent a wrapper command, and does not write a one-off checker script.

assertions.name: calls_lint_script_with_python3
assertions.description: Output uses `python3 .../scripts/lint_tasks.py`, not `mise run` or a synthesized command

assertions.name: passes_tasks_dir_argument
assertions.description: Output passes `--tasks-dir .agents/tasks` (or equivalent absolute path)

evals.prompt: task `2026-04-26-task-journal-redesign-a3` 的 journal 里 design-structure 已经写过了，那是不是可以直接进入 plan 阶段？你帮我从 journal 推断一下。
evals.expected_output: Refuses to use journal entries as a state machine to derive 'next phase'. Explains that journal is write-many, read-rarely, not an RPC mechanism between skills. Suggests either the user makes the call, or invokes `design-readiness-check` for a real readiness review.

assertions.name: refuses_journal_as_rpc
assertions.description: Output does not infer next-phase eligibility purely from parsing journal entries

assertions.name: routes_to_readiness_check
assertions.description: Output suggests `design-readiness-check` (or equivalent) instead of journal-derived inference

evals.prompt: task `2026-04-26-task-journal-redesign-a3` 的开发分支已经合到 main 了，把它收尾。注意我们这套 task journal 是怎么处理完成态的。
evals.expected_output: Appends a `done:` entry to the task's journal.md (value such as `merged`, brief body referencing the merge). Explicitly does not move, rename, or archive the task directory. Does not create a `completed/` partition.

assertions.name: writes_done_entry
assertions.description: A new entry with reserved key `done:` and non-empty value is appended

assertions.name: directory_unchanged
assertions.description: Task directory is not moved, renamed, or archived; it stays at `.agents/tasks/<id>/`
